author: PC Gamer
text:
Windows 10 gets yet another year of life as Microsoft extends security updates into 2027
Vive la Windows 10!

author: PC Gamer
text:
I picked out 31 must-play games going for $5 or less in the Steam Summer Sale
'Bout a hundo if you want them all.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Steam Machine preorder emails are in the wild, and it finally feels real: Valve is actually gonna release this thing in 2026
Did you get one?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Embark switches up Arc Raiders expeditions again, and this time it's giving away paid currency and precious blueprints
A stop gap before what sounds like larger changes to expeditions.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Meccha Chameleon review
It's wrapped in clumsy software, but it's a must-play multiplayer concept.

author: PC Gamer
text:
As its much newer sequel struggles, the 13-year-old Payday 2 is getting a 'massive engine upgrade' that improves performance and cuts the install size in half
The studio that took over Payday 2 development in 2025 has been hard at work.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Rockstar's decision to make GTA 6 fully digital is a terrible, anti-consumer move that makes me worry about the future of videogames
An increasing digital landscape shouldn't erase physical media entirely.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dispatch cleared the way for medieval narrative RPG Sovereign Tower as its developer says the line up was 'actually perfect for us'
Who wouldn't want to take charge of a magical tower and a medieval kingdom?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Haunted Chocolatier fans don't have fresh screenshots to fawn over because Eric Barone says sharing them mid-development 'feels like serving half-baked bread'
There's plenty of screenshots to go around already.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Battlefield 6 is overhauling its gunplay next week, and it's adding one thing we've all asked for years on end
Battlefield Studios has announced a series of updates looking at every aspect of combat.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Star Wars: Galactic Racer has a lot more Slay the Spire DNA than I expected, because its ex-Burnout devs wanted to add 'real consequence' to each race
High speed racing and roguelike unlocks are wrapped up in a Star Wars story.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Fallout: New Vegas director Josh Sawyer says the NCR and Caesar's Legion feel so real because their members disagree and challenge each other: 'You have to wrestle with the fact that it contains multitudes'
Shoutout to Canyon Runner.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Vampire: The Masquerade's D&D crossover is a condescending cave-in to people who are afraid of trying something new
The d10s can't hurt you, I swear.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Looks like there's no firm favourite for 2026's GOTY just yet, though there are two standouts
Still plenty of time for new contenders to enter the ring, at least.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Resident Evil 2 fans end 'years of searching' for identity of Jill Valentine's photo boyfriend
Turns out he's a small Mexican chihuahua.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I've hunted down every confirmed GTA 6 gun in its trailers and screenshots so far
There are tons of pistols, an obligatory rocket launcher, and a couple of Red Dead Redemption 2 references.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Eric Barone gives players a small update on Haunted Chocolatier's progress: 'It’s not a simple task, as I torture myself over every last detail'
Good things come to those who wait.

author: PC Gamer
text:
D&D and Magic owner Hasbro is reportedly trying to get child actors from Peppa Pig to sign away their voices to AI
This doesn't bode too well for Hasbro's videogames, either.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Down with tactical rocks
Warhammer miniatures already have bases to stand on, they don't always need to be hopping around on a bunch of rocks too.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Nightdive's Thief remaster is adding a weapon wheel and other UI changes, 'but we're not forcing you to use it'
Optional improvements are the best kind of improvements.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Amid mass layoffs at Bungie, the official White House X account issues sociopathic Destiny 2 meme
B2 bombers, a Rolls Royce, and a 4700 power level.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Generational talent just gone': Developers react in shock at the brutal scale of Bungie layoffs, which has reportedly seen the Destiny 2 team almost completely wiped out
Many of those being let go are showing incredible dignity in the face of a gigantic squandering of talent.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Deltarune Chapter 5 launches to nearly 300,000 concurrent players on Steam, Chapter 6 is coming in 2027
People sure seem to like Deltarune.

author: PC Gamer
text:
1 year after launch, spaceship shooter Wildgate will end development after its next update
Thankfully, it's not shutting down, and developer Moonshot Games says no layoffs are planned.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I've played countless Doom engine games, so believe me when I say this one that combines the FPS with JRPG combat might be the coolest one in 30 years
Free Doom total conversion End of Starchild is part bullet hell, part surprise RPG, all enigma.

author: PC Gamer
text:
PC Gamer's 2025 GOTY is yours for a ludicrous $24 during the Steam Summer Sale
Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 is 60% off, and Warhorse is celebrating with a free quest.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I dare you to refuse these unprecedented Prime Day deals on miniature shrubbery
Don't look away. I know what you are.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The best farm sim since Stardew Valley is on sale right before its 1.0 launch
Fields of Mistria is leaving Early Access in August, so now is a perfect time to grab it at a discount.

author: PC Gamer
text:
It's not layoffs, but you can see them from here: Numerous Compulsion Games employees are looking for new jobs
The sudden influx of "open to work" posts suggests that fears of major Xbox layoffs in the near future are well founded.

author: PC Gamer
text:
It's wild of Rockstar to ask us for $80, minimum, without showing a GTA 6 gameplay trailer
I really didn't think they'd open pre-orders without a new trailer, but here we are.
